parental.example: Parental lying can hurt a child's trust.

lying.definition: Saying things that are not true.
lying.example: Lying to children can damage their instincts.

secrets.definition: Things that are hidden and not told to others.
secrets.example: Family secrets can affect a child's behaviour.

withholding.definition: Keeping something back and not sharing it.
withholding.example: Withholding information is a form of lying.

consciously.definition: In a way that you are aware of and thinking about.
consciously.example: Even if family members are not consciously aware, they can sense it.

straightforward.definition: Simple, honest, and without hidden meaning.
straightforward.example: Children can feel when something is not straightforward.

omitting.definition: Leaving something out; not including it.
omitting.example: Omitting important information is a kind of lie.

dulling.definition: Making something less sharp or less strong.
dulling.example: Lying to children is dulling their instincts.

instincts.definition: Natural feelings that help you know something without thinking.
instincts.example: Trust your child's instincts about how things feel.

sensing.definition: Feeling or noticing something without being told directly.
sensing.example: Children are sensing something different from what you tell them.

discomfort.definition: A feeling of being uneasy or not comfortable.
discomfort.example: The child's discomfort came out as bad behaviour.

surface.definition: To appear or come out, especially after being hidden.
surface.example: Hidden feelings are likely to surface in behaviour.

phenomenon.definition: Something that happens or exists, especially something unusual.
phenomenon.example: This case study teaches about this phenomenon.

psychotherapist.definition: A trained professional who helps people with mental and emotional problems through talking.
psychotherapist.example: They went to see a psychotherapist about their son.

truant.definition: Staying away from school without permission.
truant.example: He was playing truant from school.

sullen.definition: Quiet and angry; refusing to talk or be friendly.
sullen.example: The teenager was sullen and uncommunicative.

uncommunicative.definition: Not willing to talk or share information.

stealing.definition: Taking something that belongs to someone else without permission.
stealing.example: He had been stealing from his mother's bag.

toe the line.definition: To follow the rules and behave properly.
toe the line.example: They wanted advice on how to get him to toe the line.

adolescence.definition: The time of life between being a child and being an adult, usually ages 12-18.
adolescence.example: During adolescence, teenagers try to separate from parents.

separate.definition: To become independent and different from someone.
separate.example: Teenagers feel a need to separate from their parents.

tribe.definition: A group of people who share something in common.
tribe.example: Teenagers form their own new tribe of friends.

identity.definition: Who you are; the qualities that make you different from others.
identity.example: They need to establish a separate identity from their parents.

stilted.definition: Stiff and unnatural, not flowing easily.
stilted.example: Their description of his childhood sounded stilted and bland.

bland.definition: Lacking interest; plain and not exciting.
bland.example: The story they told was bland and without details.

glanced.example: They glanced at each other nervously.

prompted.definition: Encouraged or pushed someone to say or do something.
prompted.example: The therapist prompted them to share more.

stern.definition: Very serious and strict, not friendly.
stern.example: His wife shot him a stern look.

womanized.definition: Had many romantic relationships with women, often unfaithfully.
womanized.example: The father had womanized and was an alcoholic.

alcoholic.definition: A person who cannot stop drinking alcohol.
alcoholic.example: His real father was an alcoholic.

genes.definition: Parts of your body's cells that control what you look like and how you grow, passed from parents.
genes.example: They worried the behaviour was in his genes.

omission.definition: The act of leaving something out.
omission.example: Not telling the truth is lying by omission.

furious.example: When they told him the truth, he was furious.

rupture.definition: A break or serious damage in a relationship.
rupture.example: They needed to repair the rupture with their son.

owning up.definition: Admitting that you did something wrong.
owning up.example: They needed to own up to preferring a perfect picture.

acknowledging.definition: Accepting and recognizing that something is true or real.
acknowledging.example: They needed to acknowledge the impact on their son.

apologizing.definition: Saying you are sorry for something you did wrong.
apologizing.example: Apologizing and accepting his feelings was necessary.

terrified.example: It is not their feelings that are a problem but our being terrified of them.

soothe.definition: To calm someone down and make them feel better.
soothe.example: If they are worried, you can soothe them.

reassurance.definition: Words or actions that help someone feel less worried.
reassurance.example: Bad news needs to be given with reassurance.

desperately.definition: In a very strong, extreme way because of great need.
desperately.example: Although we feel desperately sad now, life will go on.

bereavement.definition: The sadness you feel when someone you love dies.
bereavement.example: We cannot protect children from bereavement.

hitherto.definition: Until now; up to this point in time.
hitherto.example: The parent who had hitherto lived in the home was moving out.

predictable.definition: Happening in a way that you can expect and prepare for.
predictable.example: Children need a regular and predictable routine.

age-appropriate.definition: Suitable for a child of a particular age.
age-appropriate.example: Share the truth in an age-appropriate way.

adopted.definition: Legally taken into a family that is not your birth family.
adopted.example: If your child is adopted, tell them from the start.

calamities.definition: Terrible events that cause great harm or suffering.
calamities.example: We cannot protect children from calamities.

alongside.definition: Next to and together with someone.
alongside.example: We can be alongside our children when bad things happen.

inevitable.definition: Certain to happen; cannot be avoided.
inevitable.example: Difficult times are inevitable in life.

leisure.definition: Free time to relax and enjoy yourself.
leisure.example: Parents need leisure to enjoy being with their children.

interactions.definition: Times when people communicate or do things together.
interactions.example: Love is shown in the give and take of your interactions.
